title: The mother and father theory
wikitext:
[]: The "mother and father" theory?             Quote Post by DummyPlug » Wed Apr 15, 2009 4:24 pm I remember that when I fist started watching Evangelion -a long time ago-, I used to think that Lilith and Adam were sort of mother and father to all humans (and angels too, I think but I wasn't sure) and then I don't know at what point I found the Wiki and realized that the consensus was different, and obviously for good reasons. I'm just curious, did you believe the same at first? Or did you ever consider the possibility at some point?

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Apr 15, 2009 4:27 pm There is plenty of previous discussion on this. This is just gonna cause a shit storm. I have my own feelings on the matter but like I said, shit storm's a comin'. To answer your question, it may have been one of many possibilities that ran through my head but I never really thought that's how it was.

[]:             Quote Post by DummyPlug » Wed Apr 15, 2009 4:35 pm Oh, ehrm sorry. I couldn't find any of it in the Wiki section on misconceptions so I was curious about wether or not I was the only one who lived a lie during the entire tv series. Didn't mean to cause a $h!t storm.

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Wed Apr 15, 2009 4:56 pm its understandable, Eva's good at causing misconceptions and mindfucks lol.

[]: Re: The "mother and father" theory?             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Apr 15, 2009 5:13 pm DummyPlug wrote: I used to think that Lilith and Adam were sort of mother and father to all humans (and angels too, I think but I wasn't sure)

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Apr 15, 2009 5:26 pm my first episode was the day tokyo 3 stood still, the line that stuck in my head for a long time was Shinji saying " aren't angels messengers of god? why are we fighting them?" or something like that. this made me think for a long time that we were being punished by god for something and that we were fighting for our right to survive, so i was half right

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Apr 15, 2009 7:23 pm I wasn't really sure what to think about Lilith and Adam, really, until reading stuff on the forums. All I knew at first that they were and are freaking creepy, being mothers of their respective offspring or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 15, 2009 8:46 pm DummyPlug wrote: I couldn't find any of it in the Wiki section on misconceptions so I was curious about wether or not I was the only one who lived a lie during the entire tv series.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Apr 15, 2009 9:28 pm Hmm... never thought Misato killed Kaji, I always thought the term "Angel" and "God" were being misused or interpreted in some different manner, I did have some confusion on ego, but that developed out of forum talk, didn't think on it much directly after watching it. I took the statement of "Adam being the first man" literally, which wasn't wrong, what I was wrong about was the context I thought of it in, which I believe was unvoidable, "culture clash" and all that jazz. ... Oh right, after watching the very first episode, the line "frozen Unit-00" caused me to think two things, first, the adjective formed an image in my mind that they were "digging" the EVAs out of glaciers/ice in general, and that the EVAs were in fact an alien/lost technology. Second, since Unit-00 had the "zero" in its name, had yet to be revealed, and was above all the prototype , that it would turn out to be the most powerful of all the EVAs (e.g. Gundam Wing , which truth be told I hadn't even watched yet). But of course, by the end of episode 6, I had dropped both of these assumptions.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Apr 15, 2009 10:00 pm Alaska Slim wrote: "Adam being the first HUman"

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Apr 15, 2009 10:58 pm when they first introduce adam hes an embryo tho, so at that point i actually thought that it was the real thing in a biblical sense. It just got some details wrong

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Apr 15, 2009 11:00 pm I think everyone thought that. You say "Adam" and "first human" and everyone in the western world thinks the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 15, 2009 11:05 pm Though if you know what's good for you, you'll quickly turn around and wonder what the hell an aborted embryo that looks kind-of-human-but-not-quite in a suitcase with biohazard symbols on it has to do with Genesis-Adam. Lol anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Apr 15, 2009 11:13 pm For longest time I thought Misato killed Kaji. And that Asuka was 14 when she first showed up. And that the giant on the cross was Jesus. And that Shinji only fapped over Asuka once. And that Hyuga wasn't really a black guy disguised as a Japanese man.

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Apr 16, 2009 12:04 am my friend and i were watchin the dub of EoE the other day every time Hyuga spoke he started cracking up. it was pretty funny.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Apr 16, 2009 1:18 pm I remember thinking that Adam gave birth to Lilith, who gave birth to Man, but everything ultimately rooted back to Adam, who was created by God, who was totally stoned.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Apr 16, 2009 4:20 pm That was probably because of a Manga Entertainment's translation error in EoE that said Lilith came from Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Thu Apr 16, 2009 11:09 pm when i first watched eva i had no idea what was going on. for some time me and my brother thought the angels were only attacking us because the humans attacked them first. many years later and I still dont know much of whats going on. but i can pretend to understand. Gendo created Rei as a sextoy right.
